evals.prompt: Look up the company Acme Corp in our CRM and create a deal for them worth $50k in the qualified stage.
evals.expected_output: Searches for 'Acme Corp' using `ac crm search`, gets the company ID from results, then creates a deal with `ac crm deals create` using --name, --stage qualified, --amount 50000, and --company-id.

assertions.name: searches_crm_first
assertions.text: Runs `ac crm search` or `ac crm companies list` to find the company before creating the deal

assertions.name: creates_deal_with_correct_flags
assertions.text: Uses `ac crm deals create` with --stage qualified, --amount 50000, and --company-id

assertions.name: checks_auth_first
assertions.text: Runs `ac whoami` before executing CRM commands to verify authentication

evals.prompt: I have a JSON file at /tmp/contacts.json with a list of people to import. Bulk upsert them into our CRM.
evals.expected_output: Uses `ac crm people bulk-upsert --file /tmp/contacts.json` to import the people. May use --json flag for structured output.

assertions.name: uses_bulk_upsert_command
assertions.text: Runs `ac crm people bulk-upsert` with --file /tmp/contacts.json

assertions.name: installs_cli_first
assertions.text: Runs `pip install --upgrade agencycore-cli` before using the CLI

evals.prompt: Draft a cold email to Jane Smith at Acme Corp. She's the VP of Sales. Make it a compose email, not a reply.
evals.expected_output: Uses `ac crm comms generate-draft` with --mode compose, --recipient-name 'Jane Smith', --company-name 'Acme Corp', and --recipient-title 'VP of Sales'.

assertions.name: uses_generate_draft
assertions.text: Runs `ac crm comms generate-draft` with --mode compose

assertions.name: includes_recipient_title
assertions.text: Uses --recipient-title flag (not --context) for the VP of Sales detail

assertions.name: includes_company_name
assertions.text: Uses --company-name 'Acme Corp' or similar

evals.prompt: Add 3 prospects (IDs: p1, p2, p3) to our Q2 Enterprise outreach sequence (ID: seq-123).
evals.expected_output: Uses `ac envoy recipients add seq-123 --prospect-ids p1,p2,p3` to add the prospects.

assertions.name: uses_prospect_ids_flag
assertions.text: Uses --prospect-ids (or -p) flag with comma-separated IDs, not --source with raw JSON

assertions.name: targets_correct_sequence
assertions.text: Uses sequence ID seq-123 in the command

evals.prompt: Check my outreach inbox for open replies, read the messages in the first thread, and then reply saying we'd love to schedule a call.
evals.expected_output: Runs `ac envoy inbox list --status open` to see open threads, `ac envoy inbox messages <thread-id>` to read a thread, then `ac envoy inbox reply <thread-id> --body '...'` to reply.

assertions.name: lists_open_inbox
assertions.text: Runs `ac envoy inbox list` with --status open

assertions.name: reads_thread_messages
assertions.text: Runs `ac envoy inbox messages` with a thread ID

assertions.name: replies_to_thread
assertions.text: Runs `ac envoy inbox reply` with --body containing the reply

evals.prompt: I uploaded a CSV for workflow wf-abc. Show me the companies it discovered and add the first batch to our CRM.
evals.expected_output: Runs `ac workflows run-companies list wf-abc` to see discovered companies, then uses `ac workflows run-companies add-to-crm wf-abc --company-ids <ids>` to add them.

assertions.name: lists_discovered_companies
assertions.text: Runs `ac workflows run-companies list wf-abc` to view discovered companies

assertions.name: adds_to_crm
assertions.text: Runs `ac workflows run-companies add-to-crm` with --company-ids

assertions.name: uses_json_for_parsing
assertions.text: Uses --json flag on the list command to extract company IDs for the add-to-crm step

evals.prompt: Workflow wf-xyz found some people. Preview company matches for person IDs p1 and p2, then add them to CRM.
evals.expected_output: Runs `ac workflows run-people company-match-preview wf-xyz --person-ids p1,p2` to preview matches, then `ac workflows run-people add-to-crm wf-xyz --person-ids p1,p2`.

assertions.name: previews_company_match
assertions.text: Runs `ac workflows run-people company-match-preview` with --person-ids p1,p2

assertions.name: adds_people_to_crm
assertions.text: Runs `ac workflows run-people add-to-crm` with --person-ids

evals.prompt: Give me an analytics overview for this month and then drill into AI usage by model.
evals.expected_output: Runs `ac admin analytics-overview --start-date 2026-04-01` for the overview, then `ac admin ai-usage by-model --start-date 2026-04-01` for model breakdown.

assertions.name: uses_analytics_overview
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin analytics-overview` with appropriate date range

assertions.name: drills_into_ai_by_model
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin ai-usage by-model` to see per-model breakdown

evals.prompt: Onboard a new customer: Jane Smith (jane@newcorp.com) at NewCorp Inc. Website is https://newcorp.com. Then send them the onboarding link.
evals.expected_output: Uses `ac admin onboarding create` with all details, then `ac admin onboarding send-link <org-id> --send-email`.

assertions.name: creates_onboarding
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin onboarding create` with --email, --first-name, --last-name, --org-name, and --website-url

assertions.name: sends_link
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin onboarding send-link` with --send-email flag

evals.prompt: List our messaging sessions and show me which environments I'm logged into.
evals.expected_output: Runs `ac messaging sessions` to list sessions and `ac env list` to show environments.

assertions.name: lists_messaging_sessions
assertions.text: Runs `ac messaging sessions` to show active sessions

assertions.name: lists_environments
assertions.text: Runs `ac env list` to show all environments and login status

evals.prompt: Train a new writing style called 'Enterprise Formal' with a sample email I'll provide: 'Dear Mr. Johnson, I am writing to follow up on our recent conversation regarding the enterprise partnership...'
evals.expected_output: Creates a style with `ac styles create --name 'Enterprise Formal'`, then trains it with `ac styles train <style-id> --sample-text '...'`.

assertions.name: creates_style
assertions.text: Runs `ac styles create` with --name 'Enterprise Formal' or similar

assertions.name: trains_with_sample
assertions.text: Runs `ac styles train` with --sample-text containing the provided text

evals.prompt: Parse this CSV file at /tmp/leads.csv and show me what companies it found. Use JSON output so I can pipe it.
evals.expected_output: Runs `ac workflows csv-parse /tmp/leads.csv --json` to parse the CSV and output structured JSON.

assertions.name: uses_csv_parse
assertions.text: Runs `ac workflows csv-parse /tmp/leads.csv`

assertions.name: uses_json_flag
assertions.text: Includes --json flag for piping output

evals.prompt: Create a follow-up call task for the deal abc-123, due next Friday, high priority. Then mark it complete after I've done it.
evals.expected_output: Creates activity with `ac crm activities create --type call --title '...' --deal-id abc-123 --due-date <next-friday> --priority high`, then completes it with `ac crm activities complete <activity-id>`.

assertions.name: creates_activity_with_type
assertions.text: Uses `ac crm activities create` with --type call

assertions.name: links_to_deal
assertions.text: Uses --deal-id abc-123 to link the activity to the deal

assertions.name: sets_priority
assertions.text: Uses --priority high

assertions.name: completes_activity
assertions.text: Uses `ac crm activities complete` with the activity ID

evals.prompt: Create a static list called 'Q2 Targets' for people, then add person p-001 and person p-002 to it.
evals.expected_output: Creates list with `ac crm lists create --name 'Q2 Targets' --member-type person --type static`, then adds members with `ac crm lists add-member <list-id> --person-id p-001` and `ac crm lists add-member <list-id> --person-id p-002`.

assertions.name: creates_list
assertions.text: Uses `ac crm lists create` with --name, --member-type person, and --type static

assertions.name: adds_members
assertions.text: Uses `ac crm lists add-member` with --person-id for each person

evals.prompt: Show me our email engagement metrics for the last 60 days. I want to see open rates and click rates.
evals.expected_output: Runs `ac crm engagement-dashboard --period 60` to show engagement metrics.

assertions.name: uses_engagement_dashboard
assertions.text: Runs `ac crm engagement-dashboard` (not just `ac crm dashboard`)

assertions.name: sets_period
assertions.text: Uses --period 60 to cover 60 days

evals.prompt: Set up a new outreach sequence called 'Enterprise Q3' with our formal writing style (ID: style-abc) and playbook (ID: pb-123). Add two message steps and a 3-day delay between them, then launch it with workflow wf-999.
evals.expected_output: Creates sequence, adds steps in order (message, delay, message), then launches with the workflow ID.

assertions.name: creates_sequence
assertions.text: Uses `ac envoy sequences create` with --name, --writing-style-id, and --playbook-id

assertions.name: creates_message_steps
assertions.text: Uses `ac envoy steps create` with --type message at least twice

assertions.name: creates_delay_step
assertions.text: Uses `ac envoy steps create` with --type delay, --delay-value 3, --delay-unit days

assertions.name: launches_sequence
assertions.text: Uses `ac envoy sequences launch` with --workflow-id wf-999

evals.prompt: Check my outbox for pending drafts, then approve the first one and reject the second one asking for a regeneration because it's too formal.
evals.expected_output: Lists pending drafts, approves one with `ac envoy outbox approve`, rejects another with `ac envoy outbox reject --action regenerate_draft --reason '...'`.

assertions.name: lists_pending
assertions.text: Runs `ac envoy outbox pending` to see pending drafts

assertions.name: approves_draft
assertions.text: Runs `ac envoy outbox approve` with a draft ID

assertions.name: rejects_with_regenerate
assertions.text: Runs `ac envoy outbox reject` with --action regenerate_draft and --reason mentioning 'formal'

evals.prompt: Schedule workflow wf-daily to run every weekday at 9 AM Eastern time. Then preview the next 5 run times to make sure it looks right.
evals.expected_output: Creates schedule with cron expression for weekdays at 9 AM ET, then previews upcoming runs.

assertions.name: creates_schedule
assertions.text: Uses `ac workflows schedules create` with --cron for weekday mornings and --timezone America/New_York

assertions.name: previews_runs
assertions.text: Uses `ac workflows schedules preview` with --count 5

evals.prompt: The queues look unhealthy. Check queue health, show me the failed jobs in the 'workflows' queue, and retry them all.
evals.expected_output: Checks health, lists failed jobs, retries all.

assertions.name: checks_queue_health
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin queues health`

assertions.name: lists_failed_jobs
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin queues failed workflows` to see failed jobs in the workflows queue

assertions.name: retries_all
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin queues retry-all workflows`

evals.prompt: Find the user with email jane@acme.com, then impersonate them so I can debug their issue.
evals.expected_output: Searches for user by email, then impersonates them.

assertions.name: searches_user
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin users search --email jane@acme.com` or `ac admin users auth-search --email jane@acme.com`

assertions.name: impersonates_user
assertions.text: Runs `ac admin users impersonate <user-id>`

evals.prompt: Upload our new company logo from ./logo.png to the organization logos category, then install the 'analytics-pro' app for our org.
evals.expected_output: Uploads image then installs app.

assertions.name: uploads_image
assertions.text: Uses `ac files images upload ./logo.png --category organization_logos`

assertions.name: installs_app
assertions.text: Uses `ac apps install analytics-pro`

evals.prompt: Upload a PDF called product-guide.pdf to our knowledge base with the name 'Product Guide' and tags 'docs,product'. Then check its processing status.
evals.expected_output: Uploads resource then checks status.

assertions.name: uploads_resource
assertions.text: Uses `ac resources upload product-guide.pdf --name "Product Guide" --tags "docs,product"`

assertions.name: checks_status
assertions.text: Uses `ac resources status <resource-id>` to check processing

evals.prompt: Delete the 3 people with IDs p-100, p-101, p-102 from the CRM in one go. Skip confirmations since I'm scripting this.
evals.expected_output: Uses bulk-delete with AC_YES or --yes flag.

assertions.name: uses_bulk_delete
assertions.text: Uses `ac crm people bulk-delete --ids p-100,p-101,p-102`

assertions.name: skips_confirmation
assertions.text: Uses --yes flag or AC_YES=1 to skip confirmation prompts

evals.prompt: I need to switch to the production environment, check if the API is healthy, and then look up my profile.
evals.expected_output: Switches env, checks health, views profile.

assertions.name: switches_environment
assertions.text: Uses `ac env use production` to switch environments

assertions.name: checks_health
assertions.text: Uses `ac health check` to verify the API

assertions.name: views_profile
assertions.text: Uses `ac profiles me` to view the user's profile
